content: If the oil pressure warning light stays on or flashes while driving, pull over safely and turn off the engine immediately. Check the oil level. If the level is low, add oil before restarting. If the light remains on, do not restart; call for roadside assistance.

A red brake warning light indicates a potential fault in the braking system or that the parking brake is engaged. Ensure the parking brake is fully released. If the light persists, have the system inspected immediately by a qualified technician.

The TPMS light illuminates when one or more tires are significantly under-inflated. Check all tire pressures and adjust to the recommended PSI found on the driver-side door jamb. The light will turn off after driving a short distance.

A solid Check Engine light indicates a problem with the vehicle's emissions system. Driving is typically safe, but you should have it inspected soon. A flashing Check Engine light indicates a severe misfire that could damage your catalytic converter; reduce speed and seek immediate service.

If this light illuminates while driving, the battery is not charging. Turn off unnecessary electrical accessories (AC, radio) and drive immediately to a service center. If the vehicle stalls, do not attempt to restart.

If the temperature gauge enters the red zone, safely pull over and allow the engine to cool. Do not open the hood or radiator cap while the engine is hot. Check coolant levels once cooled and top up if necessary.
If your vehicle won't start and you hear a clicking sound, your battery may be drained. You can attempt to jump-start the vehicle using a second car and jumper cables, or contact roadside assistance.
If the vehicle does not detect the key fob, place it in the backup slot (usually located in the center console or glove box) to allow the vehicle to start. This may indicate a low battery in the fob.

ACC maintains a set speed and a set distance between your vehicle and the vehicle in front. It uses radar and camera sensors. If the sensors are blocked by dirt, snow, or rain, the system may deactivate.
The Lane-Keeping System uses a camera mounted behind the rearview mirror to monitor lane markings. It provides steering assistance or vibration alerts if you unintentionally drift out of your lane.
BlueCruise allows for hands-free highway driving on pre-qualified sections of divided highways called Hands-Free Blue Zones. A driver-facing camera ensures you are keeping your eyes on the road.
If the infotainment screen freezes, you can perform a soft reset by holding the Volume Down and Seek Forward buttons on the steering wheel simultaneously for 10 seconds.

Pro Power Onboard allows you to use your truck as a mobile generator. Depending on the model, it can provide up to 9.6 kW of power. Use the outlets in the bed for high-draw tools. Monitor power usage via the SYNC 4 screen or FordPass app.

Your Ford vehicle receives OTA updates periodically, typically every 2-4 months or as critical improvements become available. These updates enhance SYNC 4 features, safety systems, and vehicle performance. Ensure your vehicle is connected to Wi-Fi for faster downloads. You can schedule updates for a specific time when the vehicle is not in use.

In vehicles with electronic door latches, such as the Mustang Mach-E, there is a mechanical emergency release lever located on the door panel. Pull this lever fully to manually open the door if electrical power is lost.
To set your own 5-digit code, enter the factory default code, press 1-2 on the keypad, enter your new 5-digit code, and finally press 1-2 again to save it for driver 1 or 3-4 for driver 2.

The Pro Trailer Backup Assist allows you to steer the trailer with a knob on the instrument panel while the vehicle controls the steering wheel. You must first calibrate the system by following the on-screen prompts while driving straight.

A family of turbocharged, direct-injection gasoline engines designed by Ford to deliver horsepower and torque consistent with those of larger-displacement engines, while achieving better fuel efficiency.
Ford's hands-free highway driving technology that allows for hands-free operation on pre-qualified sections of divided highways.
Goes Over Any Type of Terrain. A management system in the Ford Bronco that adjusts steering, handling, and powertrain response to suit different off-road conditions.
Ford's latest infotainment system, featuring cloud connectivity, wireless smartphone integration (Apple CarPlay/Android Auto), and enhanced voice recognition.

The Lower Anchors and Tethers for CHildren (LATCH) system makes it easier to install child safety seats. Locate the anchors in the seat bight and attach the seat connectors. Secure the top tether to the anchor behind the seat.

If the fuel door fails to open electrically, locate the manual release pull-cable behind the access panel in the trunk or cargo area (usually on the same side as the fuel door). Pull the cable to release the latch.

TCS helps prevent wheel spin by reducing engine power or applying brakes to individual wheels. You can temporarily disable it (e.g., when stuck in deep snow) using the TCS button or via the infotainment settings.

When jump-starting, always connect the positive (+) lead to the positive terminal of the dead battery first, then to the good battery. Connect the negative (-) lead to the good battery, and finally to a solid, unpainted metal ground point on the engine block of the dead vehicle—NOT the negative terminal of the dead battery.
ESC uses sensors to monitor the vehicle's direction and helps the driver maintain control during cornering or sudden maneuvers by applying brakes to individual wheels and adjusting engine torque.

Trailer Reverse Guidance uses cameras in the sideview mirrors and visual cues on the touchscreen to help you stay in control while backing up a trailer. It provides multiple views and auto-tracking to keep the trailer hitch in sight.

Your Ford vehicle is equipped with an IOLM that calculates oil change intervals based on engine operating conditions. It will alert you via the instrument cluster when service is required. Do not exceed one year or 10,000 miles between changes.

MyKey allows you to program keys with restricted vehicle modes to promote good driving habits. You can limit top speeds, set seatbelt reminders that mute the audio system, and set permanent safety feature activations.

On equipped Ford trucks and SUVs, you can operate the power tailgate via the instrument panel button, the key fob, or by pressing the release button on the tailgate itself. Ensure the area is clear before operation.
